title: Ixora korthalsiana
taxonomy: Ixora korthalsiana is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to Southeast Asia, specifically in Indonesia and Malaysia. The plant is classified within the genus Ixora, which comprises around 400 species of flowering plants. The Ixora genus is further classified under the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the coffee or madder family. The classification of Ixora korthalsiana reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Rubiaceae family.
uses: Ixora korthalsiana has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine. The plant's roots and leaves are employed in folk remedies to treat various ailments. In traditional medicine, it is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Ixora korthalsiana is also used to treat fever, digestive issues, and skin conditions. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of its medicinal uses.

intro: Ixora korthalsiana, also known as the Malay tea, is a shrub-like plant that belongs to the Rubiaceae family, native to tropical Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Ixora korthalsiana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include infusions and decoctions, often consumed as a tea to harness its potential health benefits, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
